def: A push or pull acting on a body, measured in newtons.
ex: The applied {{force}} causes the beam to deflect downward.

def: Internal force per unit area within a deformable body under load.
ex: The {{stress}} at the fixed end exceeds the allowable limit.

def: Ratio of change in length to original length under stress.
ex: The measured {{strain}} increases linearly within the elastic region.

def: Twisting moment that tends to rotate a body about an axis.
ex: The motor delivers enough {{torque}} to turn the loaded shaft.

def: Internal moment causing a beam to bend about its cross-section.
ex: The maximum {{bending moment}} occurs at the centre of the span.

def: Internal force acting parallel to a beam's cross-section.
ex: The {{shear force}} changes sign directly under the applied load.

def: Axial pulling force that tends to stretch a member.
ex: The cable is in {{tension}} while supporting the suspended mass.

def: Axial pushing force that tends to shorten a member.
ex: The vertical column carries the roof load in {{compression}}.

def: Ability of a material to return to its original shape.
ex: Steel's high {{elasticity}} lets it recover after the load is removed.

def: Tendency of a material to deform permanently under load.
ex: Beyond the yield point, {{plasticity}} produces irreversible deformation.

def: Stress at which a material begins to deform permanently.
ex: The design stress stays well below the material's {{yield strength}}.

def: Maximum tensile stress a material can withstand before fracture.
ex: The alloy's {{tensile strength}} determines the maximum safe rope load.

def: Progressive material damage caused by repeated cyclic loading.
ex: Cyclic vibration led to {{fatigue}} cracking near the weld.

def: Change in shape or size of a body under load.
ex: Excessive {{deformation}} of the panel indicates an overloaded structure.

def: Displacement of a structural element under an applied load.
ex: The beam's {{deflection}} must not exceed one three-hundredth of the span.

def: Resistive force opposing relative motion between contacting surfaces.
ex: Static {{friction}} keeps the block stationary on the inclined plane.

def: Study of motion without considering the forces causing it.
ex: The {{kinematics}} of the linkage defines each joint's velocity.

def: Product of a body's mass and its velocity.
ex: Conservation of {{momentum}} governs the two-body collision.

def: State in which all forces and moments on a body balance.
ex: The truss is in static {{equilibrium}} when net force equals zero.

def: External force or weight applied to a structure or component.
ex: The distributed {{load}} on the floor is fifteen kilonewtons per metre.

def: Structural member designed primarily to resist bending loads.
ex: The steel {{beam}} spans between two masonry supports.

def: Beam fixed at one end and free at the other.
ex: The balcony is built as a {{cantilever}} projecting from the wall.

def: Sudden sideways failure of a slender member under compression.
ex: The slender column failed by {{buckling}} before reaching yield stress.

def: Ratio of tensile stress to strain in the elastic range.
ex: Steel has a {{Young's modulus}} of about two hundred gigapascals.

def: Ratio of a component's strength to its actual load.
ex: A {{factor of safety}} of three protects against unexpected overloads.

def: Point where a body's entire weight is considered concentrated.
ex: A low {{centre of gravity}} improves the vehicle's rollover stability.

def: Geometric property describing resistance to bending or rotation.
ex: A larger {{moment of inertia}} reduces the beam's deflection under load.

def: Rotating machine element that transmits torque and power.
ex: The drive {{shaft}} transfers torque from the engine to the wheels.

def: Stress acting tangentially to a material's cross-sectional plane.
ex: The bolt fails when the {{shear stress}} exceeds its shear capacity.

def: Stress acting perpendicular to a material's cross-sectional plane.
ex: Axial loading produces a uniform {{normal stress}} across the section.

def: Force exerted by a support to keep a body balanced.
ex: Each support provides a vertical {{reaction force}} to carry the load.

def: Sketch showing all external forces acting on a body.
ex: Draw a {{free body diagram}} before writing the equilibrium equations.

def: Framework of members joined at nodes carrying axial loads.
ex: The roof {{truss}} distributes the load through triangular members.

def: Structural member designed to resist compressive axial loads.
ex: The diagonal {{strut}} carries compression between the two joints.

def: Structural member designed to resist tensile axial loads.
ex: The horizontal {{tie}} prevents the arch from spreading outward.

def: Force applied along the longitudinal axis of a member.
ex: The column carries a pure {{axial load}} with no bending.

def: Rate of change of velocity of a moving body.
ex: The net force equals mass times {{acceleration}} by Newton's law.

def: Rate of change of position with direction, measured vectorially.
ex: The piston's {{velocity}} is zero at the top of its stroke.

def: Resistance of a body to changes in its motion.
ex: A heavy flywheel has large {{inertia}} and resists speed changes.

def: Energy possessed by a body due to its motion.
ex: The rotating flywheel stores {{kinetic energy}} for later release.

def: Stored energy due to a body's position or configuration.
ex: The raised weight gains {{potential energy}} equal to force times height.

def: Energy transferred when a force moves through a distance.
ex: The {{work}} done by the force equals force times displacement.

def: Rate at which work is done or energy transferred.
ex: The engine delivers a peak {{power}} of one hundred kilowatts.

def: Increase in length of a member subjected to tension.
ex: The bar's {{elongation}} under load was measured with a strain gauge.

def: Ability of a material to stretch before it fractures.
ex: The steel's high {{ductility}} allows large deformation before failure.

def: Tendency of a material to fracture with little deformation.
ex: Cast iron's {{brittleness}} causes sudden failure without warning.

def: Resistance of a component to elastic deformation under load.
ex: Increasing the section depth raises the beam's bending {{stiffness}}.

def: Resistance of a material's surface to indentation or scratching.
ex: The gear teeth are heat-treated to increase their surface {{hardness}}.

def: Energy a material absorbs before fracturing under load.
ex: Impact tests measure the {{toughness}} of the welded joint.

def: Slow permanent deformation under constant load over time.
ex: At high temperature the turbine blade suffers gradual {{creep}}.

def: Separation of a material into pieces under applied stress.
ex: The brittle {{fracture}} propagated rapidly across the plate.

def: Single force equivalent to a system of combined forces.
ex: The {{resultant}} of the two forces acts along the diagonal.

def: Two equal opposite forces producing pure rotation, no translation.
ex: The two forces form a {{couple}} that twists the shaft.

def: Ratio relating friction force to the normal contact force.
ex: A low {{friction coefficient}} lets the surfaces slide easily.

def: Maximum stress a material sustains without permanent deformation.
ex: Beyond the {{elastic limit}} the material no longer springs back.

def: Maximum stress a material withstands before it ruptures.
ex: The specimen reached its {{ultimate strength}} just before breaking.

def: Load spread continuously over a length or area.
ex: The snow acts as a uniform {{distributed load}} on the roof.

def: Load concentrated at a single point on a member.
ex: A {{point load}} at midspan produces the largest bending moment.

def: Point where a structure is held and reactions develop.
ex: The pinned {{support}} allows rotation but prevents translation.

def: Vertical member that carries compressive loads to the foundation.
ex: Each {{column}} transfers the floor load down to the footing.

def: Twisting of a member caused by an applied torque.
ex: The shaft experiences {{torsion}} when the load is applied off-axis.

def: Quantity having both magnitude and direction, like force.
ex: Each force is drawn as a {{vector}} with length and direction.

def: Quantity with magnitude only and no direction.
ex: Mass is a {{scalar}}, whereas velocity is a vector.

def: Force that attracts bodies toward the earth's centre.
ex: The weight of the beam results from {{gravity}} acting on its mass.

def: Gravitational force acting on a body's mass.
ex: The bridge must support its own {{weight}} plus traffic loads.

def: Amount of matter in a body, resisting acceleration.
ex: The block's {{mass}} determines its inertia during acceleration.

def: Force applied perpendicularly over a unit of area.
ex: The hydraulic {{pressure}} pushes the piston along the cylinder.

def: Turning effect of a force about a point.
ex: The {{moment}} of the force about the hinge equals force times distance.

def: Idealized body that does not deform under applied loads.
ex: In statics we treat the frame as a {{rigid body}}.

def: Ratio of lateral strain to axial strain under load.
ex: Most metals have a {{Poisson's ratio}} of about zero point three.

def: Ratio of shear stress to shear strain in a material.
ex: The shaft's twist depends on its {{shear modulus}} and length.

def: Load applied suddenly over a very short time.
ex: A dropped hammer produces a severe {{impact load}} on the anvil.

def: Geometric centre of an area or cross-section.
ex: The neutral axis passes through the section's {{centroid}}.

def: The movement of thermal energy from a hotter region to a colder one
ex: Engineers analyze {{heat transfer}} to keep the engine from overheating

def: Heat transfer through a solid material by direct molecular contact
ex: Metal handles get hot through {{conduction}} along the material

def: Heat transfer by the bulk movement of a heated fluid or gas
ex: Radiators warm a room mainly through natural {{convection}} of air

def: Heat transfer through electromagnetic waves without a material medium
ex: The sun heats the Earth by thermal {{radiation}} across empty space

def: A measure of disorder or unavailable energy in a thermodynamic system
ex: The second law states that {{entropy}} of an isolated system increases

def: Total heat content of a system at constant pressure
ex: The change in {{enthalpy}} determines the heat released during combustion

def: A chemical reaction between fuel and oxygen releasing heat and light
ex: Internal {{combustion}} engines burn fuel to produce mechanical power

def: The increase in size of a material when its temperature rises
ex: Bridges include gaps to allow for {{thermal expansion}} in summer

def: Heat needed to raise one kilogram of a substance by one degree
ex: Water's high {{specific heat}} makes it an excellent coolant

def: The total kinetic and potential energy of molecules within a system
ex: Adding heat raises the {{internal energy}} of a confined gas

def: A fluid's resistance to flow caused by internal friction
ex: Honey has a higher {{viscosity}} than water at room temperature

def: Smooth fluid motion in parallel layers without mixing between them
ex: At low speeds the pipe carries water in steady {{laminar flow}}

def: Chaotic fluid motion with irregular eddies and rapid mixing
ex: High velocity in the duct produces {{turbulent flow}} and pressure loss

def: The study of forces and motion in flowing liquids and gases
ex: {{Fluid dynamics}} helps engineers design efficient aircraft wings

def: Faster fluid flow corresponds to lower pressure within the stream
ex: {{Bernoulli's principle}} explains how airflow generates lift on a wing

def: A device that moves fluids by mechanical action and adds pressure
ex: The centrifugal {{pump}} raises water from the well to the tank

def: A rotary machine that extracts energy from a moving fluid flow
ex: Steam drives the {{turbine}} that spins the electrical generator

def: A machine that increases the pressure of a gas by reducing volume
ex: The {{compressor}} feeds high-pressure air into the jet engine

def: A device transferring heat between two fluids without mixing them
ex: The {{heat exchanger}} cools engine oil using circulating coolant

def: A closed vessel that heats water to produce steam or hot water
ex: The power plant {{boiler}} generates steam for the turbines

def: A thermodynamic loop that removes heat to cool a space
ex: The {{refrigeration cycle}} uses a refrigerant to absorb indoor heat

def: The ratio of useful work output to heat energy input
ex: Improving {{thermal efficiency}} reduces the fuel a plant consumes

def: The upward force a fluid exerts on a submerged or floating body
ex: {{Buoyancy}} keeps the steel ship afloat despite its weight

def: The volume or mass of fluid passing a point per unit time
ex: The valve controls the {{flow rate}} of coolant through the system

def: A measure of the average kinetic energy of particles in matter
ex: The sensor logs the {{temperature}} at the reactor outlet

def: The science of heat, work, and energy transformations in systems
ex: {{Thermodynamics}} governs how engines convert fuel into motion

def: The amount of heat needed to raise a body's temperature by one degree
ex: A large water tank has a high {{heat capacity}} and warms slowly

def: A thermodynamic change occurring with no heat entering or leaving
ex: Rapid compression in the cylinder is nearly an {{adiabatic process}}

def: A thermodynamic change taking place at constant temperature
ex: During an {{isothermal process}} the gas exchanges heat with surroundings

def: Heat absorbed or released during a phase change at constant temperature
ex: Evaporating water absorbs {{latent heat}} and cools the surface

def: Heat that changes a substance's temperature without a phase change
ex: Warming air adds {{sensible heat}} that a thermometer can measure

def: The gas or liquid that carries energy through a thermal cycle
ex: Steam serves as the {{working fluid}} in a Rankine power plant

def: An idealised thermodynamic cycle giving the maximum possible efficiency
ex: The {{Carnot cycle}} sets the theoretical limit for engine efficiency

def: A device converting thermal energy into mechanical work
ex: A car motor is a {{heat engine}} burning fuel to move pistons

def: A material property measuring how well it conducts heat
ex: Copper's high {{thermal conductivity}} makes it ideal for heat sinks

def: A material that reduces heat flow between regions of different temperature
ex: Foam {{insulation}} keeps the pipe from losing heat to the air

def: The gaseous phase of a substance below its critical temperature
ex: Boiling water releases {{vapour}} that drives the turbine

def: The change of a vapour into liquid when it loses heat
ex: {{Condensation}} in the condenser turns exhaust steam back to water

def: The change of a liquid into vapour at its surface
ex: {{Evaporation}} of refrigerant absorbs heat inside the cooling coil

def: The mass of a substance contained in a unit of volume
ex: Warm air has lower {{density}} and therefore rises

def: The upward force equal to the weight of displaced fluid
ex: The {{buoyant force}} supports a submarine at neutral depth

def: Pressure exerted by a fluid at rest due to gravity
ex: {{Hydrostatic pressure}} rises with depth beneath the water surface

def: A dimensionless ratio predicting whether flow is laminar or turbulent
ex: A high {{Reynolds number}} indicates the pipe flow is turbulent

def: A line showing the direction of fluid velocity at each point
ex: Each {{streamline}} traces the path a fluid particle follows

def: The resisting force opposing an object moving through a fluid
ex: Streamlined shapes reduce aerodynamic {{drag}} on a vehicle

def: The upward aerodynamic force perpendicular to the flow direction
ex: The wing generates {{lift}} that keeps the aircraft airborne

def: Formation and collapse of vapour bubbles in a low-pressure liquid
ex: {{Cavitation}} damages pump impellers by eroding the metal surface

def: A narrowing duct that increases a fluid's speed as it exits
ex: The rocket {{nozzle}} accelerates exhaust gases to high velocity

def: A widening passage that slows a fluid and raises its pressure
ex: The {{diffuser}} converts fast airflow into higher static pressure

def: The thin fluid region near a surface where velocity changes sharply
ex: Friction within the {{boundary layer}} causes drag on the wing

def: The volume occupied by one unit mass of a substance
ex: Steam tables list the {{specific volume}} of vapour at each pressure

def: A theoretical gas whose particles have no volume or attraction
ex: The {{ideal gas}} law relates pressure, volume, and temperature

def: A transition of matter between solid, liquid, and gaseous states
ex: Boiling is a {{phase change}} from liquid water into vapour

def: The temperature at which a liquid boils under a given pressure
ex: At atmospheric pressure the {{saturation temperature}} of water is 100 degrees

def: Heating a vapour above its saturation temperature at a given pressure
ex: {{Superheating}} the steam raises the efficiency of the turbine

def: A fluid that carries heat away from a hot component
ex: The engine circulates {{coolant}} to prevent it from overheating

def: A working fluid that absorbs and releases heat in a cooling cycle
ex: The {{refrigerant}} evaporates inside the coil to absorb heat

def: Water in its gaseous state produced by boiling
ex: High-pressure {{steam}} spins the blades of the power turbine

def: Internal stress in a material caused by temperature-induced expansion
ex: Uneven heating produces {{thermal stress}} that can crack the casting

def: The rate of heat transfer through a unit area of surface
ex: Engineers limit the {{heat flux}} across the reactor wall

def: The mass of fluid passing a section per unit time
ex: The {{mass flow rate}} of fuel controls the engine's power output

def: Pressure measured relative to the surrounding atmospheric pressure
ex: The tyre shows a {{gauge pressure}} of two hundred kilopascals

def: Pressure measured relative to a perfect vacuum reference
ex: Steam tables use {{absolute pressure}} rather than gauge readings

def: A component that absorbs and dissipates heat from a device
ex: The finned {{heat sink}} draws heat away from the processor

def: A device that transfers heat from a fluid to the surrounding air
ex: The car's {{radiator}} cools the coolant before it returns

def: The volume of fluid passing a point per unit time
ex: The flowmeter reports the {{volumetric flow rate}} in litres per second

def: The energy released when a unit of fuel is completely burned
ex: Diesel has a higher {{heat of combustion}} than petrol per litre

def: A device that lowers refrigerant pressure before the evaporator
ex: The {{expansion valve}} drops the refrigerant pressure and temperature

def: A heat exchanger that turns vapour into liquid by removing heat
ex: The {{condenser}} rejects heat and liquefies the refrigerant

def: A heat exchanger where a liquid absorbs heat and becomes vapour
ex: Cold air forms as refrigerant boils inside the {{evaporator}}

def: A thermodynamic cycle used in steam power plants
ex: Most steam power stations operate on the {{Rankine cycle}}

def: The discharge of waste heat from a system to its surroundings
ex: The cooling tower handles {{heat rejection}} from the power plant

def: Toothed wheel that meshes with another to transmit rotation and torque
ex: The {{gear}} meshes with the pinion to reduce output speed.

def: Component that supports a rotating shaft while reducing friction between parts
ex: A worn {{bearing}} produces vibration and noise in the spindle.

def: Sliding component moving inside a cylinder to transmit combustion or pressure force
ex: The {{piston}} moves up and down within the engine cylinder.

def: Shaft converting reciprocating piston motion into rotational output in engines
ex: The {{crankshaft}} converts the piston's linear motion into rotation.

def: Shaft with lobes that opens and closes engine valves in sequence
ex: The {{camshaft}} times the opening of the intake valves.

def: Device controlling flow of fluid, gas, or air through a passage
ex: The intake {{valve}} admits the air-fuel mixture into the cylinder.

def: Heavy rotating wheel storing rotational energy to smooth speed fluctuations
ex: The {{flywheel}} stores energy and steadies the engine's rotation.

def: Device joining two shafts together to transmit torque between them
ex: A flexible {{coupling}} connects the motor shaft to the pump.

def: Mechanism engaging or disengaging power transmission between rotating shafts
ex: The driver pressed the {{clutch}} to change gears smoothly.

def: Device converting energy into controlled mechanical motion or force
ex: A hydraulic {{actuator}} extends the arm to lift the load.

def: Rotating profile that imparts reciprocating motion to a follower
ex: The {{cam}} lifts the follower once per revolution.

def: Elastic element that stores energy and returns to its shape
ex: The compression {{spring}} absorbs shock and returns the valve.

def: Hardware element joining components together, such as bolts or screws
ex: Each {{fastener}} must be torqued to the specified value.

def: Threaded fastener used with a nut to clamp parts together
ex: Tighten the {{bolt}} until it reaches the required torque.

def: Threaded block that mates with a bolt to secure a joint
ex: Thread the {{nut}} onto the bolt and tighten firmly.

def: Joint formed by fusing metal parts together with heat
ex: Inspect the {{weld}} for cracks before loading the frame.

def: Part produced by pouring molten metal into a shaped mould
ex: The engine block is a single aluminium {{casting}}.

def: Metal part shaped by compressive forces such as hammering or pressing
ex: The crankshaft is a steel {{forging}} for high strength.

def: Removing material with cutting tools to achieve final dimensions
ex: Precise {{machining}} produces the required surface finish.

def: Machine that rotates a workpiece against a tool for turning
ex: The operator turned the shaft on the {{lathe}}.

def: Machine using rotating cutters to remove material from a workpiece
ex: The slot was cut on a vertical {{milling machine}}.

def: Computer numerical control automating machine tool motion from programmed instructions
ex: The part was produced on a {{CNC}} machining centre.

def: Permissible variation of a dimension from its nominal value
ex: The bore must be machined within a tight {{tolerance}}.

def: Applying oil or grease to reduce friction and wear
ex: Proper {{lubrication}} extends the life of the bearings.

def: System transmitting power through pressurised fluid to drive actuators
ex: The {{hydraulic system}} raises the platform under heavy load.

def: System using compressed air to transmit power and drive components
ex: The {{pneumatic system}} actuates the clamping cylinders.

def: Enclosed set of gears altering speed and torque between shafts
ex: The {{gearbox}} reduces motor speed for the conveyor drive.

def: Assembly transmitting power from engine to driven components at varying ratios
ex: The {{transmission}} delivers torque to the wheels.

def: Grooved wheel carrying a belt or rope to transmit motion
ex: The belt runs over the drive {{pulley}} to the fan.

def: Flexible loop transmitting motion between two or more pulleys
ex: The V-{{belt}} drives the alternator from the crankshaft.

def: Series of linked elements transmitting power between sprockets
ex: The roller {{chain}} drives the rear sprocket.

def: Toothed wheel engaging a chain to transmit rotary motion
ex: The chain wraps around the driven {{sprocket}}.

def: Cylindrical liner reducing friction and wear between moving parts
ex: A bronze {{bushing}} supports the rotating pin.

def: Sealing element placed between surfaces to prevent leakage
ex: Replace the cylinder head {{gasket}} to stop the oil leak.

def: Component preventing fluid leakage between stationary or moving parts
ex: The oil {{seal}} keeps lubricant inside the housing.

def: Chamber in which a piston moves under pressure or combustion
ex: The {{cylinder}} guides the piston during each stroke.

def: Rotating part of a machine such as a motor or turbine
ex: The {{rotor}} spins inside the stator windings.

def: Stationary part of a machine surrounding the rotating rotor
ex: The {{stator}} generates the magnetic field for the motor.

def: Support member fixing or holding a component to a structure
ex: The motor is bolted to a steel {{bracket}}.

def: Enclosure protecting and supporting internal mechanical components
ex: The gears run inside a sealed aluminium {{housing}}.

def: System of connected links transmitting force and constraining motion
ex: The four-bar {{linkage}} converts rotation into oscillation.

def: Internal cylindrical hole machined into a component
ex: The cylinder {{bore}} was honed to the correct diameter.

def: Helical ridge on a bolt or hole enabling threaded fastening
ex: The bolt {{thread}} matched the tapped hole exactly.

def: Slot cut into a shaft or hub to seat a key
ex: The key sits in the {{keyway}} to prevent slipping.

def: Series of ridges on a shaft engaging a matching hub
ex: The {{spline}} transmits torque between the shaft and hub.

def: Projecting rim used to bolt or connect components together
ex: The pipe {{flange}} is bolted to the pump inlet.

def: Thin ring distributing load under a bolt or nut
ex: Place a {{washer}} under the nut to spread the load.

def: Permanent fastener deformed to join two plates together
ex: Each {{rivet}} is set to clamp the sheet-metal panels.

def: Small gear driving a larger gear or a rack
ex: The {{pinion}} drives the rack to move the slide.

def: Gear set using a screw meshing with a wheel for high reduction
ex: The {{worm gear}} gives a large speed reduction in one stage.

def: Threaded shaft converting rotary motion into precise linear movement
ex: The {{lead screw}} advances the table on the machine.

def: Controlled heating and cooling to alter metal properties
ex: {{Heat treatment}} hardens the gear teeth against wear.

def: Heating and slow cooling to soften metal and relieve stress
ex: {{Annealing}} softens the steel before further machining.

def: Rapid cooling of heated metal to increase its hardness
ex: {{Quenching}} in oil hardens the tool steel.

def: Abrasive machining producing fine finish and precise dimensions
ex: {{Grinding}} produces the smooth surface on the bearing journal.

def: Cutting round holes into material using a rotating drill
ex: {{Drilling}} produces the mounting holes in the plate.

def: Cutting internal threads inside a drilled hole with a tap
ex: {{Tapping}} creates the threads for the securing bolt.

def: Process of fitting components together to build a product
ex: Final {{assembly}} joins the gearbox to the motor.

def: Free play between mating gear teeth before motion is transmitted
ex: Excessive {{backlash}} in the gears reduces positioning accuracy.

def: Mounted unit enclosing a bearing to support a rotating shaft
ex: The {{housing bearing}} carries the conveyor drive shaft.

def: Substance reducing friction and wear between moving surfaces
ex: Apply a thin film of {{lubricant}} to the guide rails.

def: Relationship of clearance or interference between mating machined parts
ex: An interference {{tolerance fit}} holds the bearing on the shaft.

def: Cylindrical liner guiding or protecting a shaft against wear
ex: The {{bushing sleeve}} guides the plunger inside the bore.

def: Precise cylindrical pin locating two parts accurately together
ex: A {{dowel pin}} aligns the two halves of the mould.

def: Frequency at which a system oscillates freely without any external driving force
ex: Every mechanical structure has a characteristic {{natural frequency}} of vibration.

def: Large amplitude oscillation when driving frequency matches the natural frequency
ex: Bridge failure can occur when wind induces dangerous {{resonance}}.

def: Dimensionless measure describing how oscillations decay after a disturbance
ex: A high {{damping ratio}} quickly suppresses unwanted vibrations.

def: Periodic oscillation where restoring force is proportional to displacement
ex: A pendulum approximates simple {{harmonic motion}} at small angles.

def: Independent coordinate needed to fully define a system's configuration
ex: A rigid body in space has six {{degree of freedom}} components.

def: Rotational quantity equal to inertia times angular velocity of a body
ex: A spinning skater conserves {{angular momentum}} while pulling arms inward.

def: Spinning device maintaining orientation by resisting changes to its axis
ex: The aircraft's {{gyroscope}} stabilises the navigation instruments during flight.

def: Rotational speed at which a shaft resonates and vibrates violently
ex: Operating below the {{critical speed}} prevents excessive shaft whirling.

def: Correcting mass distribution so a rotating part runs without vibration
ex: Precise {{dynamic balancing}} of the rotor eliminates bearing wear.

def: Control path returning output information to adjust the system input
ex: The temperature controller relies on a fast {{feedback loop}}.

def: Control that continuously corrects output using measured feedback signals
ex: A robotic arm uses {{closed-loop control}} for accurate positioning.

def: Control acting without feedback, ignoring the actual system output
ex: A basic washing machine timer is {{open-loop control}}.

def: Controller combining proportional, integral and derivative terms for stable regulation
ex: The {{PID controller}} tunes the motor speed to the setpoint.

def: Automatic device using feedback to control position, speed or force
ex: A precise {{servomechanism}} drives the machine tool axis.

def: Ratio expressing system output relative to input in frequency domain
ex: Engineers derive the {{transfer function}} to analyse system response.

def: Residual difference between desired and actual output after transients settle
ex: Adding integral action reduces the {{steady-state error}} to zero.

def: Device automatically maintaining constant engine speed under varying load
ex: The steam engine {{governor}} keeps the speed nearly constant.

def: Reducing transmission of vibration between a machine and its support
ex: Rubber mounts provide effective {{vibration isolation}} for the compressor.

def: Maximum displacement of an oscillating body from its equilibrium position
ex: The vibration sensor records the peak {{amplitude}} of motion.

def: Number of oscillation cycles completed by a system per second
ex: The shaker table sweeps through a range of {{frequency}} values.

def: Time taken to complete one full cycle of oscillation
ex: The pendulum's {{period}} depends only on its length.

def: Angular measure of the offset between two oscillating quantities
ex: The {{phase angle}} between force and displacement indicates damping.

def: Oscillation sustained by a continuously applied external periodic force
ex: An unbalanced motor produces steady {{forced vibration}} in the frame.

def: Oscillation of a system after an initial disturbance without external forcing
ex: After the impact the beam undergoes decaying {{free vibration}}.

def: Component that dissipates energy to reduce oscillation amplitude over time
ex: A hydraulic {{damper}} absorbs the shock of the suspension.

def: Piston-in-fluid device providing velocity-dependent resistance to damp motion
ex: The valve mechanism includes a {{dashpot}} to slow closure.

def: Characteristic value from which a system's natural frequencies are obtained
ex: Each {{eigenvalue}} of the matrix yields a vibration mode.

def: Deformation pattern of a structure vibrating at a given natural frequency
ex: The first {{mode shape}} shows maximum deflection at midspan.

def: Study determining the natural frequencies and mode shapes of a structure
ex: Engineers performed {{modal analysis}} before finalising the chassis design.

def: Target value that a control system tries to maintain
ex: The thermostat drives the temperature toward the {{setpoint}}.

def: Ratio by which a controller amplifies its input signal
ex: Increasing the proportional {{gain}} speeds up the response.

def: Frequency range over which a system responds effectively to input
ex: A wide control {{bandwidth}} allows fast disturbance rejection.

def: Amount by which a response exceeds its final steady value
ex: Excessive {{overshoot}} indicates the controller needs more damping.

def: Time for a response to stay within tolerance of its final value
ex: Tuning the gains shortened the {{settling time}} considerably.

def: Time for a response to rise between specified low and high levels
ex: A faster {{rise time}} improves the actuator's responsiveness.

def: Tendency of a system to return to equilibrium after disturbance
ex: Feedback design ensures the control system's long-term {{stability}}.

def: Condition where oscillations grow without bound instead of decaying
ex: Too much gain can drive the loop into {{instability}}.

def: Device measuring a physical quantity and converting it to a signal
ex: A position {{sensor}} feeds data to the controller.

def: Device converting one form of energy or signal into another
ex: The pressure {{transducer}} outputs a proportional voltage.

def: Instrument measuring acceleration, widely used in vibration monitoring
ex: An {{accelerometer}} mounted on the housing records the vibration levels.

def: Instrument measuring the rotational speed of a shaft or wheel
ex: The {{tachometer}} displays the engine speed in revolutions per minute.

def: Rate at which an object rotates about an axis per unit time
ex: The turbine's {{angular velocity}} increases as steam pressure rises.

def: Rate of change of angular velocity with respect to time
ex: Applying torque produces {{angular acceleration}} in the flywheel.

def: Inward force keeping a body moving along a circular path
ex: The {{centripetal force}} on the rotor rises with speed.

def: Apparent outward force experienced by a body in rotating motion
ex: {{Centrifugal force}} throws the governor weights outward at high speed.

def: Slow rotation of a spinning body's axis around another axis
ex: Gyroscopic {{precession}} causes the wheel axis to slowly rotate.

def: Oscillation component whose frequency is a multiple of the fundamental
ex: The signal analyser detects a strong second {{harmonic}}.

def: Graphical shape of a signal plotted against time
ex: The oscilloscope displays the vibration {{waveform}} clearly.

def: Distribution of signal amplitude across its component frequencies
ex: The frequency {{spectrum}} reveals a dominant peak at resonance.

def: Method decomposing a signal into its constituent sinusoidal frequencies
ex: {{Fourier analysis}} separates the noise into individual frequency components.

def: System's output when excited by a very short input pulse
ex: The {{impulse response}} characterises the structure's dynamic behaviour.

def: System's steady output amplitude and phase versus input frequency
ex: The {{frequency response}} shows a sharp peak near resonance.

def: Graph of gain and phase against frequency on logarithmic scales
ex: The {{Bode plot}} confirms adequate stability margins for the loop.

def: Additional phase lag tolerable before a control loop becomes unstable
ex: A {{phase margin}} of forty-five degrees ensures robust stability.

def: Factor by which loop gain may increase before instability occurs
ex: The design maintains a comfortable {{gain margin}} against disturbances.

def: Control action proportional to the instantaneous error signal
ex: Simple {{proportional control}} leaves a small residual offset.

def: Control component summing past error to eliminate steady offset
ex: Adding {{integral action}} removes the persistent temperature error.

def: Control component responding to the rate of error change
ex: {{Derivative action}} anticipates changes and reduces overshoot.

def: Valve adjusting fluid flow in response to a control signal
ex: The {{control valve}} modulates steam flow to the turbine.

def: Coil producing magnetic force to actuate a plunger or valve
ex: The {{solenoid}} opens the valve when energised.

def: Motor with feedback control for precise position or speed
ex: A {{servo motor}} positions the robotic joint accurately.

def: Motor rotating in discrete steps for accurate open-loop positioning
ex: The printer uses a {{stepper motor}} to advance the paper.

def: Sensor converting shaft position or motion into digital signals
ex: A rotary {{encoder}} reports the exact angle of the shaft.

def: Switch that signals when a moving part reaches a boundary
ex: The {{limit switch}} stops the carriage at the travel end.

def: Unwanted external influence pushing a controlled variable off target
ex: The controller rejects the load {{disturbance}} within seconds.

def: Difference between the desired setpoint and the measured output
ex: The {{error signal}} drives the actuator to correct deviation.

def: Process of converting a control signal into mechanical motion
ex: Hydraulic {{actuation}} moves the heavy machine gate.

def: Adjusting controller parameters to achieve the desired dynamic response
ex: Careful {{tuning}} of the gains eliminated the oscillations.

def: Force pushing an oscillating body back toward its equilibrium position
ex: The spring's {{restoring force}} drives the mass back to centre.

def: Measure of decay rate found from successive vibration amplitude ratios
ex: The {{logarithmic decrement}} quantifies how quickly oscillations die out.

def: Bowing and rotating deflection of a shaft near critical speed
ex: Shaft {{whirling}} becomes severe as speed approaches the critical value.

def: Sustained oscillation drawing energy from a steady internal source
ex: Machine tool chatter is a form of {{self-excited vibration}}.

def: Unstable self-excited vibration between tool and workpiece during machining
ex: Excessive {{chatter}} leaves marks on the machined surface.

def: Constant relating damping force to the velocity of motion
ex: A higher {{damping coefficient}} dissipates vibration energy faster.

def: Frequency at which a driven system oscillates with maximum amplitude
ex: Operating away from the {{resonant frequency}} avoids destructive vibration.

def: Motion that repeats itself at regular fixed intervals of time
ex: The reciprocating piston exhibits smooth {{periodic motion}}.

def: Repeated back-and-forth movement about an equilibrium position
ex: The unbalanced load caused a persistent {{oscillation}} in the frame.

def: Repetitive straight-line back-and-forth movement of a machine part
ex: The engine converts {{reciprocating motion}} into rotation.

def: Motion of a body turning about a fixed axis
ex: The gearbox transmits {{rotary motion}} to the wheels.

def: Storage of rotational energy smoothing out speed fluctuations of a shaft
ex: The {{flywheel effect}} keeps the engine turning between power strokes.

def: Adjusting mass distribution to reduce vibration in rotating machinery
ex: Proper {{balancing}} of the wheel removes the steering shake.

def: Instrument measuring the amplitude and frequency of mechanical vibration
ex: A laser {{vibrometer}} measures surface vibration without contact.

def: Maintaining a controlled variable at a desired value despite disturbances
ex: Voltage {{regulation}} keeps the output steady under load changes.

def: Control anticipating disturbances by acting before they affect output
ex: {{Feedforward control}} corrects for the measured inlet disturbance early.

def: Dependence of a system's output on its history of inputs
ex: Mechanical {{hysteresis}} in the actuator introduces positioning error.

def: Forming process where material is pushed through a shaped die to create long profiles.
ex: Aluminium window frames are made by {{extrusion}} through a heated die.

def: Process of injecting molten plastic into a mould to produce identical parts rapidly.
ex: Plastic bottle caps are produced by {{injection moulding}} in high volumes.

def: Casting method forcing molten metal under pressure into a reusable metal mould.
ex: Engine housings are often produced by {{die casting}} of aluminium alloys.

def: Shaping flat metal sheets into parts through bending, drawing or pressing operations.
ex: Car body panels are made by {{sheet metal forming}} on large presses.

def: Pressing operation that cuts or shapes sheet metal using a die and press.
ex: Metal washers are produced by {{stamping}} thousands per hour.

def: Bonding powdered material into a solid mass by heating below its melting point.
ex: Ceramic components gain strength through {{sintering}} in a furnace.

def: Building parts layer by layer directly from a digital model.
ex: Complex brackets are now made by {{additive manufacturing}} instead of machining.

def: Fabrication technique that deposits material layer by layer to form objects.
ex: Prototype gears were made overnight using {{3D printing}}.

def: Removing metal using controlled electrical sparks between an electrode and workpiece.
ex: Hardened dies are cut precisely by {{electrical discharge machining}}.

def: The texture and smoothness of a machined or manufactured surface.
ex: A fine {{surface finish}} reduces friction in the bearing.

def: A metallic material made by combining two or more elements.
ex: Brass is an {{alloy}} of copper and zinc.

def: Material made from two constituents with different properties combined for performance.
ex: Aircraft wings use lightweight {{composite material}} for strength.

def: A material's ability to withstand chemical attack and surface degradation.
ex: Stainless steel offers excellent {{corrosion resistance}} in wet environments.

def: Reheating hardened steel to reduce brittleness and increase toughness.
ex: After quenching, the blade undergoes {{tempering}} to relieve stress.

def: Hardening the outer layer of a steel part while keeping a soft core.
ex: Gear teeth receive {{case hardening}} to resist surface wear.

def: The fine internal structure of a material seen under a microscope.
ex: Heat treatment changes the {{microstructure}} of the steel.

def: The interface separating two crystal grains within a metal.
ex: Cracks often start along a weak {{grain boundary}}.

def: Strengthening of a metal caused by plastic deformation during forming.
ex: Repeated bending causes {{work hardening}} of the copper wire.

def: Casting method using a sand mould to shape molten metal.
ex: Large engine blocks are made by {{sand casting}}.

def: Precision casting using a wax pattern coated with a ceramic shell.
ex: Turbine blades are formed by {{investment casting}} for accuracy.

def: Reducing metal thickness by passing it between rotating rolls.
ex: Steel sheets are produced by {{rolling}} hot slabs.

def: Forming a sheet into a deep hollow shape using a punch and die.
ex: Metal cans are made by {{deep drawing}} of thin sheet.

def: Cutting a flat piece from sheet metal where the removed part is the product.
ex: Circular discs are produced by {{blanking}} from steel strip.

def: Creating holes in sheet metal by pushing a punch through it.
ex: Ventilation holes are made by {{punching}} the panel.

def: Cutting sheet metal along a straight line without forming chips.
ex: Plates are cut to length by {{shearing}}.

def: Joining metals using heat generated by an electric arc.
ex: Steel beams are joined by {{arc welding}} on site.

def: Joining metals with a filler that melts above 450 degrees Celsius.
ex: Copper pipes are connected by {{brazing}} with a filler rod.

def: Joining metals using a filler that melts below 450 degrees Celsius.
ex: Electronic components are attached by {{soldering}} to the board.

def: Fastening parts together permanently using metal rivets.
ex: Aircraft skins are held by {{riveting}} thousands of fasteners.

def: Making parts by compacting metal powder and heating it to bond.
ex: Self-lubricating bearings are made by {{powder metallurgy}}.

def: Coating steel with zinc to protect it against corrosion.
ex: Outdoor fences receive {{galvanizing}} to prevent rust.

def: Depositing a thin metal layer onto a surface using electric current.
ex: Bathroom taps are finished by chrome {{electroplating}}.

def: Electrochemical process that thickens the oxide layer on aluminium.
ex: Coloured aluminium parts are made by {{anodizing}}.

def: Treatment forming a protective film that reduces surface reactivity.
ex: Stainless steel undergoes {{passivation}} to restore corrosion resistance.

def: Heat treatment refining grain structure by air cooling from high temperature.
ex: Forged parts are given {{normalizing}} to improve uniformity.

def: Adding carbon to a steel surface to increase its hardness.
ex: Camshafts are strengthened by {{carburizing}} the surface.

def: Hardening a steel surface by diffusing nitrogen into it.
ex: Crankshafts are treated by {{nitriding}} for wear resistance.

def: Deforming metal below its recrystallization temperature to increase strength.
ex: Wire is strengthened by {{cold working}} during drawing.

def: Shaping metal above its recrystallization temperature for easier deformation.
ex: Large ingots are shaped by {{hot working}} in a press.

def: Formation of new strain-free grains in a deformed metal during heating.
ex: Annealing causes {{recrystallization}} that softens the cold-worked metal.

def: Hard, brittle steel phase formed by rapid cooling of austenite.
ex: Quenching transforms austenite into hard {{martensite}}.

def: High-temperature phase of steel with a face-centred cubic structure.
ex: Steel becomes {{austenite}} when heated above the critical temperature.

def: Soft, ductile phase of iron with a body-centred cubic structure.
ex: Low-carbon steel contains mostly soft {{ferrite}}.

def: Layered steel structure of alternating ferrite and cementite bands.
ex: Slow cooling produces a fine {{pearlite}} structure.

def: Hard iron carbide compound found in steel and cast iron.
ex: The hard {{cementite}} phase increases wear resistance.

def: Describing an alloy composition with the lowest melting point.
ex: The {{eutectic}} alloy solidifies at a single temperature.

def: Chart showing stable phases of an alloy at various temperatures and compositions.
ex: The {{phase diagram}} predicts which phases form on cooling.

def: The change of a material from liquid to solid state.
ex: Shrinkage occurs during {{solidification}} of the casting.

def: Tree-like crystal structure that grows during metal solidification.
ex: Rapid cooling produces a fine {{dendrite}} structure.

def: Presence of small voids or gas pockets within a material.
ex: Trapped gas caused {{porosity}} in the weld.

def: A foreign particle trapped within a metal that weakens it.
ex: A slag {{inclusion}} caused the part to crack early.

def: A line defect in a crystal lattice that enables plastic deformation.
ex: Movement of each {{dislocation}} allows the metal to bend.

def: Crystal plane along which dislocations move during deformation.
ex: Deformation occurs when atoms shift along a {{slip plane}}.

def: Regular repeating arrangement of atoms in a crystalline solid.
ex: Iron has a body-centred cubic {{lattice structure}}.

def: The ordered geometric arrangement of atoms within a crystalline material.
ex: The {{crystal structure}} determines the metal's properties.

def: A material made of long chains of repeating molecular units.
ex: Nylon is a synthetic {{polymer}} used for gears.

def: A polymer that softens when heated and hardens when cooled repeatedly.
ex: Bottles are made from {{thermoplastic}} that can be recycled.

def: A polymer that hardens permanently once cured and cannot be remelted.
ex: Epoxy is a {{thermoset}} that sets hard during curing.

def: A rubber-like polymer that returns to shape after large deformation.
ex: Seals are made from an {{elastomer}} for flexibility.

def: A hard, brittle, heat-resistant material made from non-metallic minerals.
ex: Cutting tools use {{ceramic}} for high-temperature strength.

def: Composite combining ceramic hardness with metallic toughness.
ex: Cutting inserts made of {{cermet}} last longer at high speed.

def: The continuous phase that binds reinforcement together in a composite.
ex: Carbon fibres are held in an epoxy {{matrix}}.

def: Stronger phase added to a matrix to improve composite strength.
ex: Glass fibres provide {{reinforcement}} in the plastic panel.

def: Material built from bonded layers to combine their properties.
ex: The wing skin is a carbon-fibre {{laminate}}.

def: Corrosion when two different metals contact in a conductive solution.
ex: {{Galvanic corrosion}} attacked the joint between steel and aluminium.

def: Chemical reaction of a material with oxygen forming an oxide.
ex: {{Oxidation}} of the steel surface produced a thin scale.

def: Reddish-brown iron oxide formed when steel corrodes in moisture.
ex: {{Rust}} formed on the unpainted bolt after rain.

def: Localized corrosion producing small deep holes in a metal surface.
ex: Chloride exposure caused {{pitting corrosion}} on the pipe.

def: Ability of a metal to be shaped by hammering or rolling without breaking.
ex: Gold's high {{malleability}} allows it to be beaten thin.

def: Energy a material can absorb elastically and release on unloading.
ex: Spring steel has high {{resilience}} for storing energy.

def: A material's ability to resist surface loss from rubbing contact.
ex: Hardened gears have high {{wear resistance}}.

def: Surface wear caused by hard particles rubbing against a material.
ex: Sand caused {{abrasion}} of the pump impeller.

def: Surface damage from adhesion between sliding metal parts under load.
ex: Poor lubrication led to {{galling}} on the threads.

def: A rough raised edge left on metal after cutting or machining.
ex: The sharp {{burr}} was removed before assembly.

def: A beveled edge cut at an angle between two surfaces.
ex: A small {{chamfer}} on the shaft eases assembly.

def: A rounded internal corner that reduces stress concentration.
ex: A generous {{fillet}} prevents cracking at the corner.

def: Pressing a textured pattern onto a surface to improve grip.
ex: The knob has {{knurling}} for a better grip.

def: Finishing a drilled hole to precise size and smoothness.
ex: The hole was sized accurately by {{reaming}}.

def: Fine abrasive finishing of a bore to improve accuracy and finish.
ex: Engine cylinders are finished by {{honing}}.

def: Ultra-fine finishing using loose abrasive between two surfaces.
ex: Sealing faces are smoothed by {{lapping}}.

def: Machining a profile by pushing a toothed tool across the workpiece.
ex: Internal keyways are cut by {{broaching}}.

def: Cutting a conical recess so a screw head sits flush.
ex: Screw holes are prepared by {{countersinking}}.

def: Machining a rotating workpiece with a single-point cutting tool.
ex: The shaft diameter was reduced by {{turning}} on a lathe.

def: Cutting material with a focused high-power laser beam.
ex: Steel sheets are cut precisely by {{laser cutting}}.

def: Cutting conductive metal with a jet of ionized gas.
ex: Thick plate is severed by {{plasma cutting}}.

def: Cutting material with a high-pressure stream of water and abrasive.
ex: Heat-sensitive parts are shaped by {{water jet cutting}}.

def: Shaping a heated plastic sheet over a mould using vacuum or pressure.
ex: Food trays are made by {{thermoforming}} plastic sheet.

def: Forming hollow plastic parts by inflating a heated tube inside a mould.
ex: Plastic bottles are produced by {{blow moulding}}.

def: Shaping material by pressing it between heated mould halves.
ex: Thermoset parts are made by {{compression moulding}}.

def: Treating rubber with sulphur to create cross-links and improve strength.
ex: Tyre rubber gains elasticity through {{vulcanization}}.

def: Hardening of a resin or adhesive through a chemical reaction.
ex: The epoxy needs full {{curing}} before loading.

def: A hard material used to grind, polish or cut surfaces.
ex: Grinding wheels contain a bonded {{abrasive}}.

def: How easily a material can be cut with good finish and tool life.
ex: Free-cutting steel has excellent {{machinability}}.

def: Speed at which a cutting tool advances into the workpiece.
ex: A lower {{feed rate}} gives a smoother finish.

def: Relative surface speed between a cutting tool and the workpiece.
ex: Higher {{cutting speed}} shortens tool life.

def: Gradual loss of a cutting tool's shape during machining.
ex: Excessive heat accelerates {{tool wear}}.

def: The way material shears away as a chip during cutting.
ex: Ductile metals show continuous {{chip formation}}.

def: Slight taper on a moulded part allowing easy removal from the mould.
ex: A {{draft angle}} lets the casting release cleanly.

def: The line where the two halves of a mould meet.
ex: A faint {{parting line}} is visible on the moulded part.

def: Reduction in size of a part as it cools and solidifies.
ex: The mould is oversized to allow for {{shrinkage}}.

def: Localized increase in stress around holes, notches or sharp corners.
ex: A sharp corner creates a dangerous {{stress concentration}}.

def: Rotating abrasive wheel used to remove material and finish surfaces.
ex: The worn part was restored with a {{grinding wheel}}.

def: Region near a weld where microstructure changes due to heat.
ex: Cracks appeared in the {{heat affected zone}} of the weld.

def: An engine that burns fuel inside cylinders to produce mechanical motion.
ex: The {{internal combustion engine}} powered nearly every car of the twentieth century.

def: An engine cycle with intake, compression, power and exhaust strokes.
ex: A {{four-stroke cycle}} completes in two full crankshaft revolutions.

def: Delivering fuel into an engine under pressure through calibrated nozzles.
ex: Electronic {{fuel injection}} replaced the carburettor in most modern engines.

def: An exhaust-driven device that compresses intake air to boost engine power.
ex: The {{turbocharger}} forces more air into the cylinders for extra power.

def: A belt-driven compressor that increases air pressure entering the engine.
ex: A {{supercharger}} draws power directly from the crankshaft to boost intake.

def: A branched pipe collecting exhaust gases from several cylinders.
ex: The {{exhaust manifold}} channels hot gases toward the turbine and pipe.

def: A component distributing the air-fuel mixture to engine cylinders.
ex: The {{intake manifold}} spreads air evenly across all four cylinders.

def: An exhaust device converting harmful gases into less toxic emissions.
ex: The {{catalytic converter}} reduces carbon monoxide in the exhaust stream.

def: Springs and dampers that absorb shocks between wheels and chassis.
ex: A soft {{suspension system}} smooths out bumps on rough roads.

def: A damper that controls spring oscillation for a stable ride.
ex: A worn {{shock absorber}} lets the car bounce after every bump.

def: A gear set allowing driven wheels to turn at different speeds.
ex: The {{differential}} lets the outer wheel spin faster while cornering.

def: The components transmitting engine power to the driving wheels.
ex: The {{drivetrain}} carries torque from the gearbox to the axles.

def: The engine, transmission and driveline that propel a vehicle.
ex: Engineers redesigned the {{powertrain}} for better fuel economy.

def: The ratio of input to output rotational speed in a gearbox.
ex: A lower {{transmission ratio}} raises torque but reduces top speed.

def: The structural frame supporting a vehicle's body and components.
ex: The {{chassis}} carries the engine, suspension and all body panels.

def: A device that blends air and fuel for older combustion engines.
ex: The {{carburettor}} mixes air with petrol before it enters the cylinder.

def: A part that ignites the air-fuel mixture with an electric spark.
ex: A fouled {{spark plug}} causes the engine to misfire under load.

def: The moment the spark fires relative to piston position.
ex: Advancing the {{ignition timing}} can improve power but risks knocking.

def: Abnormal combustion causing a metallic knocking sound in the engine.
ex: Low-octane fuel can trigger {{engine knock}} under heavy acceleration.

def: The top part of an engine housing valves and combustion chambers.
ex: The {{cylinder head}} seals the top of each combustion chamber.

def: The enclosed space where the air-fuel mixture burns in an engine.
ex: Fuel ignites inside the {{combustion chamber}} to push the piston down.

def: A rod linking the piston to the crankshaft in an engine.
ex: The {{connecting rod}} transfers piston force to the rotating crankshaft.

def: A belt synchronising the crankshaft and camshaft rotation.
ex: A snapped {{timing belt}} can wreck the valves in some engines.

def: A valve controlling the amount of air entering the engine.
ex: Pressing the pedal opens the {{throttle}} to admit more air.

def: The engine's rotational speed when running without any load.
ex: A rough {{idle speed}} often signals a vacuum leak somewhere.

def: The distance a vehicle travels per unit of fuel consumed.
ex: Smaller engines usually deliver better {{fuel economy}} on the motorway.

def: A pollutant gas released from a vehicle's exhaust into the air.
ex: Stricter limits force manufacturers to cut every {{emission}} from exhausts.

def: A system preventing wheel lock-up during hard braking.
ex: The {{anti-lock braking system}} keeps the wheels turning while stopping.

def: A brake using pads clamping a rotating disc to slow a wheel.
ex: The {{disc brake}} squeezes the rotor to slow the spinning wheel.

def: A housing that presses brake pads against the disc.
ex: The {{brake caliper}} pushes the pads onto the disc under pressure.

def: A shaft that carries wheels and may transmit driving torque.
ex: The rear {{axle}} delivers power to both driven wheels.

def: A geared bar converting steering wheel motion into wheel turning.
ex: The {{steering rack}} translates wheel rotation into a change of direction.

def: The grooved rubber surface of a tyre gripping the road.
ex: Worn {{tyre tread}} greatly reduces grip on wet surfaces.

def: The study of how air moves around solid bodies in motion.
ex: Good {{aerodynamics}} lowers fuel use at high road speeds.

def: A number describing a body's resistance to airflow.
ex: A sleek shape gives the car a low {{drag coefficient}}.

def: Aerodynamic force pressing a vehicle onto the road for grip.
ex: The rear wing generates {{downforce}} that keeps the car planted.

def: An aerodynamic surface disrupting airflow to reduce lift or drag.
ex: The rear {{spoiler}} improves stability at high speed.

def: The forward force propelling an aircraft or rocket through air.
ex: The jet engines produce enough {{thrust}} to lift the airliner.

def: An engine producing thrust by ejecting a high-speed exhaust jet.
ex: A modern {{jet engine}} burns kerosene to drive the aircraft forward.

def: A jet engine where all air passes through the combustion core.
ex: The {{turbojet}} was the first practical engine for high-speed flight.

def: A jet engine with a large fan bypassing air around the core.
ex: The quiet {{turbofan}} powers most modern passenger airliners.

def: A set of rotating blades producing thrust by pushing air backward.
ex: The spinning {{propeller}} pulls the light aircraft through the air.

def: The main body of an aircraft carrying crew, passengers and cargo.
ex: The {{fuselage}} houses the cabin and connects the wings and tail.

def: A curved surface shaped to generate lift from airflow.
ex: The wing's {{aerofoil}} produces lift as air flows over it.

def: The angle between a wing's chord and the oncoming airflow.
ex: Raising the {{angle of attack}} increases lift until the wing stalls.

def: The wheels and struts supporting an aircraft on the ground.
ex: The pilot lowered the {{landing gear}} before the final approach.

def: A sudden loss of lift when airflow separates from the wing.
ex: An excessive angle of attack triggers a {{stall}} and rapid sink.

def: A hinged wing surface controlling an aircraft's roll motion.
ex: Moving the {{aileron}} banks the aircraft into a turn.

def: A movable vertical tail surface controlling an aircraft's yaw.
ex: The pilot uses the {{rudder}} to keep the nose aligned.

def: A hinged horizontal tail surface controlling an aircraft's pitch.
ex: Pulling back moves the {{elevator}} and raises the aircraft's nose.

def: A hinged wing panel extended to increase lift at low speed.
ex: The crew extends each {{flap}} for a slower landing approach.

def: The distance measured from one wingtip to the other.
ex: The airliner's {{wingspan}} barely fits the airport gate.

def: The compartment from which pilots operate an aircraft.
ex: The {{cockpit}} displays airspeed, altitude and engine data.

def: The vertical height of an aircraft above sea level.
ex: The jet levelled off at a cruising {{altitude}} of ten kilometres.

def: The speed of an aircraft relative to the surrounding air.
ex: Pilots monitor {{airspeed}} closely to avoid an approaching stall.

def: The ratio of an object's speed to the speed of sound.
ex: At {{Mach number}} one, the aircraft reaches the speed of sound.

def: Travelling faster than the speed of sound in air.
ex: The {{supersonic}} jet crossed the ocean in under four hours.

def: The ratio of bypassed air to air passing through the engine core.
ex: A high {{bypass ratio}} makes a turbofan quieter and more efficient.

def: A device injecting extra fuel into exhaust to boost thrust.
ex: The pilot engaged the {{afterburner}} for a rapid climb.

def: An aerofoil blade that compresses incoming air in a jet engine.
ex: A cracked {{compressor blade}} can destroy an entire jet engine.

def: The streamlined housing that encloses and protects an aircraft engine.
ex: The engine sits inside a {{nacelle}} slung beneath the wing.

def: The tail assembly of an aircraft providing stability and control.
ex: The {{empennage}} includes the vertical fin and horizontal stabiliser.

def: A structural partition dividing sections of a fuselage or hull.
ex: The pressure {{bulkhead}} seals the rear of the passenger cabin.

def: A lengthwise stiffener supporting an aircraft's skin panels.
ex: Each {{stringer}} runs along the fuselage to stiffen the skin.

def: A transverse structural member giving a wing its aerofoil shape.
ex: Each {{rib}} maintains the wing's cross-sectional profile.

def: The main spanwise beam carrying bending loads in a wing.
ex: The main {{spar}} bears most of the wing's bending load.

def: The revenue-earning cargo or passengers a vehicle carries.
ex: A heavier {{payload}} shortens the aircraft's maximum range.

def: An engine producing thrust by ejecting propellant, needing no outside air.
ex: The {{rocket engine}} carries its own oxidiser into space.

def: The fuel and oxidiser burned to produce rocket thrust.
ex: The rocket's tanks hold liquid {{propellant}} for the main engines.

def: A measure of how efficiently a rocket uses its propellant.
ex: Higher {{specific impulse}} means more thrust per kilogram of fuel.

def: The minimum speed needed to escape a body's gravitational pull.
ex: A spacecraft must reach {{escape velocity}} to leave Earth's gravity.

def: A pivoted mount allowing an engine nozzle to swivel for steering.
ex: The engine {{gimbal}} tilts the nozzle to steer the rocket.

def: A spacecraft's return through the atmosphere, generating intense heat.
ex: The heat shield protects the capsule during {{reentry}}.

def: A protective layer shielding a spacecraft from reentry heating.
ex: The ablative {{heat shield}} chars away to carry heat off the capsule.

def: Rotation of an aircraft about its lateral axis, raising the nose.
ex: The elevator controls {{pitch}} to climb or descend.

def: Rotation of an aircraft about its vertical axis, turning the nose.
ex: The rudder controls {{yaw}} to keep the aircraft coordinated.

def: Rotation of an aircraft about its longitudinal axis, banking the wings.
ex: The ailerons control {{roll}} to bank the aircraft into a turn.

def: The air pressure inside an engine's intake manifold.
ex: High {{manifold pressure}} indicates the turbo is boosting well.

def: A heat exchanger cooling compressed intake air before the engine.
ex: The {{intercooler}} lowers charge temperature to raise engine power.

def: The total swept volume of all an engine's cylinders.
ex: A larger {{engine displacement}} usually produces more torque.

def: System that controls indoor temperature, humidity and air quality for comfort.
ex: The building's {{air conditioning}} keeps offices cool during summer heat.

def: Supply and removal of air to maintain fresh, healthy indoor conditions.
ex: Proper {{ventilation}} removes stale air and prevents moisture buildup.

def: Device that senses temperature and switches heating or cooling on and off.
ex: Set the {{thermostat}} to twenty-two degrees for the meeting room.

def: Machine that transfers heat between spaces, providing efficient heating and cooling.
ex: A {{heat pump}} moves warmth from outside air into the house.

def: Channel that carries conditioned air through a building's ventilation network.
ex: Warm air travels through the sheet-metal {{duct}} to each room.

def: Machine that cools water for distribution to air conditioning systems.
ex: The rooftop {{chiller}} supplies cold water to the building's coils.

def: Structure that rejects waste heat by evaporating water into the air.
ex: The {{cooling tower}} releases heat from the plant's condenser water.

def: Ratio of water vapour present to the maximum air can hold.
ex: Comfortable rooms keep {{relative humidity}} between forty and sixty percent.

def: Appliance that removes excess moisture from indoor air.
ex: A {{dehumidifier}} keeps the basement dry and prevents mould.

def: Study of moist air properties used in HVAC design and analysis.
ex: Engineers use {{psychrometrics}} to size cooling coils accurately.

def: Central device that conditions and circulates air through ductwork.
ex: The {{air handling unit}} filters, heats and distributes fresh air.

def: Element that traps dust and particles from passing air.
ex: Replace the clogged {{filter}} to restore proper airflow.

def: Process of removing heat to lower temperature below the surroundings.
ex: Commercial {{refrigeration}} keeps stored food safely cold.

def: Cooling a liquid refrigerant below its condensing saturation temperature.
ex: Adequate {{subcooling}} ensures liquid refrigerant reaches the expansion device.

def: Ratio of useful heating or cooling to the energy input.
ex: A higher {{coefficient of performance}} means the heat pump is more efficient.

def: Small terminal device with a fan and coil that conditions room air.
ex: Each hotel room has a {{fan coil unit}} for local temperature control.

def: Complete network of ducts distributing air throughout a building.
ex: The {{ductwork}} runs above the false ceiling in every corridor.

def: Movement of air through a space, duct or component.
ex: Restricted {{air flow}} lowers the system's cooling capacity.

def: Resistance air must overcome as a fan pushes it through ducts.
ex: High {{static pressure}} forces the fan to work harder.

def: Energy from naturally replenished sources like sun, wind and water.
ex: The country now generates half its power from {{renewable energy}}.

def: Flat device that converts sunlight directly into electrical energy.
ex: Rooftop {{solar panel}} arrays supply electricity to the whole house.

def: Semiconductor unit that produces voltage when exposed to light.
ex: Each {{photovoltaic cell}} converts a small amount of sunlight to power.

def: Machine that converts wind's kinetic energy into electrical power.
ex: The offshore {{wind turbine}} spins steadily in the coastal breeze.

def: Using less energy to perform the same useful task or output.
ex: Better insulation improved the plant's overall {{energy efficiency}}.

def: Interconnected network that transmits electricity from producers to consumers.
ex: Surplus solar power is fed back into the national {{grid}}.

def: Device generating electricity from a chemical reaction, often using hydrogen.
ex: The {{fuel cell}} produces power and emits only water vapour.

def: Organic material burned or processed to produce renewable energy.
ex: The plant burns wood {{biomass}} to generate steam and electricity.

def: Heat extracted from within the Earth for power or heating.
ex: {{Geothermal energy}} heats the town using deep underground reservoirs.

def: Electricity generated by moving water driving turbines in a dam.
ex: The dam supplies {{hydroelectric power}} to millions of homes.

def: Producing electricity and useful heat together from one fuel source.
ex: {{Cogeneration}} captures waste heat to warm nearby buildings.

def: System holding electrical energy for later use during demand peaks.
ex: {{Battery storage}} lets the solar farm supply power after sunset.

def: Maximum electrical demand on a system during a given period.
ex: Air conditioners drive the {{peak load}} on hot afternoons.

def: Ratio of real power used to the apparent power supplied.
ex: A poor {{power factor}} wastes capacity in the electrical system.

def: Total greenhouse gases produced by an activity or organisation.
ex: Switching to renewables shrinks the factory's {{carbon footprint}}.

def: Field combining mechanical, electronic and control engineering in smart systems.
ex: {{Mechatronics}} unites motors, sensors and software into one machine.

def: Programmable jointed machine that manipulates objects like a human arm.
ex: The {{robotic arm}} welds car bodies on the assembly line.

def: Tool or gripper at a robot arm's tip that performs tasks.
ex: They swapped the {{end effector}} to pick up delicate parts.

def: Number of independent motions a mechanism or robot can perform.
ex: A six {{degrees of freedom}} arm can reach almost any orientation.

def: Industrial computer that automates machinery and process control tasks.
ex: The {{programmable logic controller}} sequences the packaging line automatically.

def: Control that continuously corrects using feedback from the actual output.
ex: {{Closed loop control}} adjusts heating until the target temperature is reached.

def: Control that acts without measuring or correcting the actual output.
ex: A simple timer uses {{open loop control}} with no feedback.

def: Use of machines and control systems to operate processes automatically.
ex: Factory {{automation}} reduced manual labour on the line.

def: Powered lever that converts control signals into mechanical movement.
ex: The {{actuator arm}} swings the valve open on command.

def: Robot end tool that grasps and holds parts securely.
ex: The pneumatic {{gripper}} lifts the metal blank into the press.

def: Screen or panel through which operators monitor and control machines.
ex: The operator adjusts settings on the {{human machine interface}} touchscreen.

def: Small computer chip that runs embedded control programs in devices.
ex: A {{microcontroller}} reads the sensors and drives the motors.

def: Dedicated computer built into a machine to perform specific functions.
ex: An {{embedded system}} controls the washing machine's cycles.

def: Switch operated by an electromagnet to control a larger circuit.
ex: The control {{relay}} switches the pump motor on and off.

def: System that controls a motor's speed, torque and direction.
ex: A variable frequency {{drive}} adjusts the fan's speed smoothly.

def: Device converting direct current into alternating current for equipment.
ex: The solar {{inverter}} changes panel DC into usable AC power.

def: Path a robot's tool follows through space over time.
ex: The controller plans a smooth {{trajectory}} between the two points.

def: Series of links joined so motion can be transmitted between them.
ex: Each joint in the {{kinematic chain}} adds a degree of freedom.

def: Continuous belt or chain that moves products through a process.
ex: Parts ride the {{conveyor}} from the press to inspection.

def: Nearness of an object, often detected without physical contact.
ex: The sensor measures {{proximity}} to trigger the safety stop.

def: Cooling demand from removing moisture rather than lowering air temperature.
ex: Humid climates raise the {{latent load}} on air conditioners.

def: Cooling demand from changing air temperature without altering moisture.
ex: Solar gain through windows increases the room's {{sensible load}}.

def: Grille with adjustable louvres controlling air delivered into a room.
ex: Open the {{registered vent}} to direct more air toward the desk.

def: Capturing waste heat from exhaust air to preheat incoming fresh air.
ex: A {{heat recovery}} unit cuts heating costs in winter.

def: Cooling capacity of an air conditioner expressed in refrigeration tons.
ex: The engineer sized the unit's {{tonnage}} for the whole floor.

def: Controller that varies motor speed by changing supply frequency.
ex: A {{variable frequency drive}} saves energy by slowing the pump.

def: Electrical network using digital controls to balance supply and demand.
ex: The {{smart grid}} shifts loads to match renewable output.

def: Range between a device's maximum and minimum stable operating output.
ex: A wide {{turndown ratio}} lets the boiler match low demand.

def: Distributing electrical or mechanical demand evenly across a system.
ex: {{Load balancing}} prevents any single circuit from overheating.
